verdict: T-023 v1.1: 4 boundaries. Sharp T'<=13 is CLASS-SPECIFIC (registered shell/shell-union); operator off-shell escape N=40,T'=20 falsifies it but the universal fallback T'<=N/2<=20 survives all thresholds (20<20.5<26.2<60.4; RES-1 razor-thin R_lead(20)=0.9743). Sign-off = crystalline + SHELL-SUPPORTED. No tier flip.

claims.name: boundary1_sub_theta_min_derived
claims.detail: (coherence-indistinguishability |F[P']-F[P]| <= c_ind I^2 = MARGIN/33; sub-resolution absorbed into the Gaussian sea. DERIVED)

claims.name: boundary3_offshell_fallback_survives
claims.detail: (LATTICE-but-OFF-SHELL escape (operator: N=40, T'=20>13) FALSIFIES the sharp class pin T'<=13. But the UNIVERSAL antipodal fallback T'<=N/2 holds (C_t,C_-t disjoint => 2T'<=N; verified on samples); with N<=n_pack=40.7 => T'<=20, and 20<20.5(RES-1)<26.2(RES-5)<60.4(SC-SCOPE): R_lead(20)=0.9743<1 (razor-thin x1.026), joint(20)=x1.082>1. FALLBACK-CONTROLLED (thin), not broken)

claims.name: boundary4_nonlattice_modelling_with_fallback
claims.detail: (non-lattice/incommensurate: crystalline-order MODELLING input, softened by DR-2 decoupling T'<<_eps N^eps (R-022 T6-cond / R-023 T4). Off the critical path)

claims.name: signoff_crystalline_AND_shell_supported
claims.detail: (FOUR boundaries: 1,2 DERIVED; 3 FALLBACK-CONTROLLED (T'<=20, RES-1 thin x1.026); 4 MODELLING. The operator sign-off reduces to CRYSTALLINE + SHELL-SUPPORTED (registered shell/shell-union competitor), NOT merely crystalline -- the off-shell escape is lattice but not shell-supported. No tier flip: B1/B2 T6, B5 T5)
